figures.caption: FIG. 2. Origin of f -wave splitting. (a) Comparison of numerical fit (orange) up to k5 and the analytical low-energy expansion (green). (b) f -wave splitting divided by size of parabolic part as function of spin polarization for the lower band pair. The black arrow highlights the effect of increasing hopping t1. The green dashed line is the analytical result for t1 = t2. (c) An in-plane field B∥ gaps the nodal points on the Fermi surfaces and creates a polarization s∥. (d) The spin conductivity σs xx divided by electric conductivity σxx and spin polarization |sn,k| depends qualitatively on the f -wave splitting ∆ (1) s .
